---
title: "LRP93: Mechanism model — letter-sound (L) -> word reading (W), moderated by receptive vocabulary (R)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP93 is a **joint-readiness** interaction model. It extends [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058) —
the mechanism estimate $f^L$ of letter-sound knowledge on word reading, fit over all three
phase transitions — with a **linear moderation** by **receptive** vocabulary $R$ (`rowpvt`, ROWPVT). It is
the direct companion of [LRP71](../lrp-rli-mech-071) (which moderates the same $L \to W$ curve by
*expressive* vocabulary $E$) and of LRP94 (taught-receptive, $TR$) and LRP95 (taught-expressive,
$TE$). Together the four models ask the same question for every vocabulary measure: *do
letter-sound knowledge and vocabulary have to be high **together** for larger word-reading gains,
or does either one help on its own?*

Letter sounds enter as the nonparametric **HSGP** curve $f^L$ (as in LRP58); receptive vocabulary
enters as a standardised linear main effect plus the interaction:

$$
\eta = \dots + f^{L}(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})
       + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\, z(R)
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\, z(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})\, z(R)
$$

where $z(\cdot)$ standardises to mean 0, sd 1 over the fitted rows. Both cross-coupling
coefficients use the regularising $\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior.

::: {.callout-note title="Reading the interaction"}

$\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is the change in the letter-sound -> word-reading slope per +1 SD of
receptive vocabulary (equivalently, by the symmetry of the product term, the change in the
vocabulary slope per +1 SD of letter sounds).

- $\gamma_{\text{int}} > 0$ = **synergy**: the two skills reinforce each other, so word reading
  is highest when *both* are high (the "both above a threshold" pattern).
- $\gamma_{\text{int}} \approx 0$ = the two contribute roughly **independently** (additive; either
  being high helps).
- $\gamma_{\text{int}} < 0$ = they partly **substitute** (diminishing returns to having both).

Because $z(\operatorname{logit} L)$ is centred, $\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ is the vocabulary main effect
*at the mean of letter sounds*, and $f^L$ is the $L \to W$ effect *at the mean of $R$*; with the
interaction present, $f^L$ no longer captures the full $L$ effect across all vocabulary levels.

$L$ and $R$ are positively correlated in this cohort ($r \approx 0.55$), so the discordant
high-one/low-other children are relatively few and the interaction is correspondingly **weakly
powered** — read $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ as an exploratory description, not a precise estimate.

:::

## Adjustment set

The revised LRP58/LRP71 $L \to W$ set (#245): $\{G, A, HS, IS(\text{attend}), SP, W_{\text{pre}}\}$
— group, linear age, hearing, intervention sessions, speech production, and baseline word reading.
The moderator $R$ enters **additionally**, via its main effect and interaction only.

::: {.callout-important title="R enters as the tested moderator only"}

Conditioning on a vocabulary measure can open a collider path for the $L \to W$ main association
(the reason the plain LRP58 curve omits vocabulary adjusters), so the $L$ main slope here is read
**only jointly** with the moderation, exactly as in LRP71. $L$ and $R$ are treated as parallel
prerequisites; the interaction estimand does not require resolving whether one precedes the other.

:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Adjusted association, not causal"}
Latent general ability ($GA$) is not adjusted for and the child random intercept does not stand in
for it, so everything here — $f^L$, $\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ and $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ — is a
**latent-GA-confounded adjusted association**, never a causal effect. The randomised causal claim
lives in the ITT suite.
:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
